patch.value: MCP server: request-scoped extension registries (process-global set/clear can race under concurrent native-action requests)

runDynamicExtensionAction sets process-global active extension registries (setActiveExtensionHooks/Commands/Parsers/...) then awaits the handler, and clears them in finally. The stdio MCP server processes native-action requests sequentially today, so the per-request set+clear is safe. But the process-global pattern is pre-existing and would race if requests are ever processed concurrently (a newer request overwrites globals; a completing request's finally clears them). Follow-up: scope the active registries per-request (pass them through the call instead of mutating module-global state) so the MCP server is concurrency-safe. Surfaced by Gemini on PR #150 (server.ts:668); deferred as a separate, larger refactor (the SDK PR only added the teardown finally).
